ticket: A 22-year-old G1P0 (primigravida) presents: insomnia, guilt, inability to care for infant 6 weeks PP. Provide comprehensive obstetric assessment and management plan.
correct_answer: Diagnosis: postpartum depression. Management: SSRIs, psychotherapy, support groups.

ticket: A 39-year-old G4P3 (grand multipara, AMA) presents: steady bleeding from laceration. Provide comprehensive obstetric assessment and management plan.
correct_answer: Diagnosis: postpartum hemorrhage. Management: uterine massage, uterotonics, transfusion.

ticket: A 30-year-old G2P1 (multigravida with prior cesarean) presents: prolonged second stage, no restitution. Provide comprehensive obstetric assessment and management plan.
correct_answer: Diagnosis: shoulder dystocia. Management: McRoberts, suprapubic pressure, episiotomy.

ticket: A 28-year-old G1P0 (primigravida) presents: painful vaginal bleeding, rigid uterus at 35w. Provide comprehensive obstetric assessment and management plan.
correct_answer: Diagnosis: placental abruption. Management: emergent delivery if severe, fetal monitoring, IV access and fluids.

ticket: A 38-year-old G4P3 (grand multipara, AMA) presents: intractable vomiting, ketonuria, hypokalemia at 8w. Provide comprehensive obstetric assessment and management plan.
correct_answer: Diagnosis: hyperemesis gravidarum. Management: IV fluids, thiamine, ondansetron.

ticket: A 42-year-old G4P3 presents: intractable vomiting, ketonuria, hypokalemia at 8w. What is the MOST important fetal monitoring step?
correct_answer: B

ticket: A 18-year-old G1P0 (primigravida) presents: 3-hour GTT abnormal. Provide comprehensive obstetric assessment and management plan.

correct_answer: Diagnosis: hyperemesis gravidarum. Management: IV fluids, thiamine, ondansetron.

ticket: A 26-year-old G2P1 (multigravida) presents: painless bleeding at 32w, complete previa. Provide comprehensive obstetric assessment and management plan.
correct_answer: Diagnosis: placenta previa. Management: pelvic rest, serial US, planned cesarean.

correct_answer: B

ticket: A 22-year-old G1P0 (primigravida) presents: maternal fever 38.5, tachycardia, foul-smelling fluid after prolonged ROM. Provide comprehensive obstetric assessment and management plan.
